Biden’s visit went viral in China, with media praising his “noodle diploma

stories.headline: Trump, Xi speak ahead of talks to make relations ‘better than ever’
stories.summary: Presidents Xi Jinping and Donald Trump say they’re looking forward to US-China talks.

stories.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping used US President Donald Trump's visit to Beijing to reassure American business leaders that China remains committed to further opening its economy to foreign investment.

stories.headline: UAE denies Netanyahu office's claim of secret wartime visit as Iran dubs it 'unforgivable'
stories.summary: The UAE denied Netanyahu's office's claim of a secret wartime visit, as Tehran threatened those "colluding with Israel".

stories.headline: Europe Today: Trump meets Xi Jinping as Brussels watches nervously
stories.summary: All eyes are on Beijing, where Donald Trump has arrived alongside CEOs from some of the world’s biggest tech companies. On his first visit since 2017, the US president is meeting Chinese President Xi Jinping, with Brussels following nervously and fearing the worst.

stories.headline: Zelenskyy's former chief of staff in custody amid major corruption investigation
stories.summary: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy remains silent on the case and the allegations against Andriy Yermak, who used to be the president's most trusted ally and Ukraine’s "number two."

stories.headline: Ukraine: Russian drone and missile barrage slams Kyiv, killing at least one
stories.summary: Ukraine’s capital Kyiv was struck by a large-scale Russian drone and missile attack early on Thursday, leaving at least one person dead and 31 injured, according to local officials.

stories.summary: Senate president says Senator Ronald 'Bato' dela Rosa 'no longer in the building' where he had taken refuge.

stories.headline: Hungary's Orbán-era wartime state of emergency ended, PM Péter Magyar says
stories.summary: The emergency governance framework was first introduced by the government of previous Prime Minister Viktor Orbán in 2020 as part of measures in response to the COVID-19 pandemic.

stories.headline: Latvian Prime Minister Evika Siliņa announces shock resignation
stories.summary: Latvian Prime Minister Evika Siliņa announced on Thursday she will resign, triggering the collapse of the country's governing coalition ahead of key October parliamentary elections.

stories.headline: Watch: Silicon Valley in Beijing—should Europe be worried?
stories.summary: Donald Trump has landed in Beijing for a summit that feels less like diplomacy and more like a Silicon Valley board meeting. And he hasn’t just brought officials; he has arrived with an "AI Cabinet" to navigate a high-stakes tech cold war.

stories.headline: Late warnings and a minister’s fall: Latvia’s air-defence failures revealed
stories.summary: Latvia found itself at the centre of a national security storm last week. A series of drones crossed into its airspace, sparking not only a fire on the ground but also a political crisis that toppled the defence minister and exposed deep vulnerabilities in the Baltic state’s military readiness.

stories.headline: 'They shot my neighbour in the head' - the lakeside city traumatised by war
stories.summary: Rebel fighters and Rwandan troops are accused of committing atrocities after capturing the DR Congo city of Uvira in December.

stories.headline: Copper Retreats From Record Close as Purchases in China Slow
stories.summary: Copper retreated from a record close as the rally started to deter purchases in China, where investors were monitoring the summit between Chinese leader Xi Jinping and US President Donald Trump.

stories.headline: Britain’s Political Crisis Spooks Crucial Overseas Bond Buyers
stories.summary: Bond-market volatility sparked by the UK’s latest political crisis is serving up a reminder that the government can’t take foreign demand for granted.

stories.headline: BlackRock Fund Manager Says European Stocks Are Attractive
stories.summary: Euro-zone stocks look attractive after the recent pullback, according to BlackRock Inc’s Thomas Becker, who argues that policymakers may use the current energy crisis to drive long-awaited fiscal investment.

stories.headline: Offshore Yuan Posts Best Win Streak Since 2017 as Summit Begins
stories.summary: The yuan recorded its longest stretch of gains against the dollar in nearly a decade in offshore trading, following positive signs from the first day of a key US-China summit.

stories.headline: Officially, Marco Rubio is still banned from China. So how is he in Beijing?
stories.summary: Chinese officials are using a different transliterated character for the secretary of state's name, perhaps to allow him to visit without lifting a 2020 ban.

stories.headline: Trump praises China as he ignores question about Taiwan

stories.headline: Nepal in a bind as US-China drone war reaches Everest
stories.summary: Superpowers compete for technological foothold on world’s highest peak, pushing Nepal towards a geopolitical chessboard.

stories.headline: OPEC+ Has Plan to Complete Series of Quota Hikes, Delegates Say
stories.summary: Key OPEC+ members aim to continue a series of oil quota increases over the next few months, completing the return of a layer of halted production — if only on paper — by the end of September, delegates said.

stories.headline: Retirement Funds Should Take Private Credit, Franklin CEO Says
stories.summary: Franklin Templeton Chief Executive Officer Jenny Johnson says retirement accounts are the “best place” for private credit and equity, given the illiquidity of such investments. “You can't get your money for 10 years, so you really need to be able to withstand the illiquidity,” Johnson says on Bloomberg Television. “On the other hand, a 1% additional return can mean 20% more just over a 20-year period in your retirement.” (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: The Debt Binge That Has Alphabet Execs Working the Night Shift
stories.summary: Google is hawking bonds in dollars, euros, pounds, francs and yen.

stories.headline: Poland’s Economic Growth Miss Set to Temper Rate-Hike Bets
stories.summary: Poland’s economic growth slowed more than anticipated in the first quarter, with the fading momentum set to moderate expectations regarding interest-rate increases amid a flare-up in price pressures.

stories.headline: BlackRock Says French Equities Are ‘Attractively Priced’
stories.summary: BlackRock portfolio manager Tom Becker discusses the outlook for euro-zone equities, including in France. "The underperformance of the CAC over the last one, three years, it’s pretty material," Becker tells Bloomberg Television. "It’s a pretty cyclically-exposed index. It’s got some big energy producers, so we think there’s kind of a bottom there." (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: China’s Credit Growth Misses Expectations as New Loans Contract
stories.summary: China’s credit expansion slowed far more than expected from a year earlier in April while new loans extended by the country’s banks shrank, hurt by a seasonal slowdown in lending.

stories.headline: Appian Buys Namibia Copper Project Eyeing $400 Million Mine
stories.summary: Appian Capital Advisory, a leading mining-focused private equity firm, has acquired a copper project in Namibia as it looks to boost output of a metal that’s expected to experience rapid demand growth.

stories.headline: Space Stock Craze Comes to Europe as One UK Fund Quadruples
stories.summary: It’s not just US retail investors piling into any asset connected to space exploration. Europeans want in, too.

stories.headline: Bo French Challenges Texas Oil Regulator in GOP Runoff
stories.summary: Bo French, a hard-liner known for wild social media posts, is seeking a bigger megaphone. Big oil companies, and top officials like Greg Abbott, want to stop him.

stories.headline: Midsize Cities Held Steady as U.S. Population Growth Declined
stories.summary: New census estimates show that the nation’s largest cities saw the sharpest slowdowns in population growth over the past year amid declines in immigration and plunging birthrates.

stories.headline: Madonna, Shakira, BTS to headline World Cup final half-time show
stories.summary: Coldplay's Chris Martin will curate the show during the July 19 final at the MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, FIFA says.

stories.headline: Climbers clear path to Everest summit past giant chunk of ice

stories.headline: Passengers allowed to leave norovirus-hit cruise ship
stories.summary: Passengers on the ship showing no symptoms are allowed to leave, authorities say, after 49 people fell ill from gastrointestinal sickness.

stories.headline: House Approves Year-Round Sale of Higher-Ethanol Gas

stories.summary: The situation could still change and there might be more confirmed cases, warns the head of the World Health Organization.

stories.headline: Air India crisis deepens ahead of final Ahmedabad crash report

stories.summary: More than 40 nations are involved in the mission, which the Ministry of Defence says will begin when conditions allow.

stories.headline: Australia has some of the world's costliest homes. Will scrapping tax breaks help?
stories.summary: The government hopes reforms will help young people enter the market, while critics say it will stifle supply.

stories.headline: Indigenous Australians win record A$150m after billionaire mined without permission
stories.summary: The Yindjibarndi people had sought A$1.8bn compensation for cultural damage and economic loss.

stories.headline: BJP leader back to head India's Assam state for second time in a row
stories.summary: Himanta Biswa Sarma is seen as one of the architects of BJP's rapid growth in India's northeast.

stories.headline: Trump ally Kari Lake tapped to be US ambassador to Jamaica
stories.summary: Lake thanked President Trump for nominating her, saying on social media that Jamaica "is a country I know very well, full of incredible people".

stories.headline: EU agrees sanctions on Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
stories.summary: The EU's foreign policy chief said "extremism and violence carry consequences", while Israel branded the move "arbitrary".

stories.headline: Advocaat, 78, set for World Cup record after Curacao return
stories.summary: Dick Advocaat makes a surprise return as Curacao's head coach after his successor Fred Rutten resigned just a month out from their World Cup debut.

stories.summary: Israel and Hezbollah have continued to trade fire despite a ceasefire deal being announced last month.

stories.headline: Fears of renewed Gaza war as Hamas disarmament talks stall
stories.summary: Israeli media reports suggest Israel is preparing to resume fighting because of the impasse in talks with Hamas.
